data.narrative:
ParityQC is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Wolfgang Lechner and Magdalena Hauser. The valuation universe comprises 11 comparable companies—3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation is not applicable here. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector comparables, but filtering by stage (Series A) and tier yields insufficient datapoints—0 same-stage matches, 1 adjacent-stage match, and 0 similar-valuation-tier matches. This falls short of the minimum 3 comparables needed for reliable peer-median triangulation. An all-sector median would introduce substantial noise and misrepresent ParityQC's position within the narrower quantum subset.

Without sufficient stage-matched peers, this analysis cannot support a quantitative valuation range via traditional methods. The extreme heterogeneity within the quantum computing sector—spanning distinct technology platforms, commercialization timelines, and capital requirements—means broader peer medians would obscure material differences. Key uncertainties include ParityQC's IP defensibility relative to better-capitalized competitors, path to revenue, and timing of quantum advantage demonstration. A credible valuation here requires either (1) access to ParityQC's detailed financial projections and technical milestones, or (2) accumulation of additional Series A quantum computing exits to establish empirical precedent.
